texto: It almost felt like we were driving in our own world--like we were inside a snow globe--and there was music and sunlight and smiles and laughter floating in the air. And it was all self-contained in a beautiful bubble filled with glittering water that made things seem a little unreal, a little dream-like and hazy.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Melissa C. Walker: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a motivacao. O trecho “It almost felt like we were driving in our own world--like we were inside a snow globe--and there…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que motivacao, dream, dreamy pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de dreamy e infinite.

texto: Maybe you're getting into the rhythm of sailing life," says James. He looks out at the waves that are rolling in to lap against the dock. "You know, the tides going in and then out, the wind blowing east and then west, the high of a perfect day out on the water, the low of a thunderstorm or a wind that won't go your way.
explicacao: Citação de Melissa C. Walker sobre esforço, persistência e resultados. “Maybe you're getting into the rhythm of sailing life," says James. He looks out at the waves that…” articula reflexao, beauty, sailing em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como dia-chuvoso e sailing.

texto: You can't beat yourself up anymore,' he says. 'And you can't compare your thing to my thing or to anyone else's thing on the how-bad-should-I-feel? scale.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Melissa C. Walker que provoca temas ligados a reflexao. Ao questionar “You can't beat yourself up anymore,' he says. 'And you can't compare your thing to my thing or to…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, grief, life-lessons, com foco em luto e life-lessons.
